mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals weakening labor demand and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during labor market expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based economic strength, boosting corporate earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in GDPC1 and RSAFS fail to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60 percent of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid acceleration in the industrial production index following a macroeconomic shock indicates capacity utilization recovery, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain flat or negative during confirmed recovery phases, or the signal's Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in out-of-sample recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 4.0 percent increase discount rates and constrain liquidity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as equity valuations compress.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in over 65 percent of high-rate instances, or the negative correlation between FEDFUNDS and forward returns vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates indicates labor market tightening and wage pressure, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter due to margin compression.
failure_condition: The interaction effect fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of cases, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in new housing starts reflect rising consumer confidence and construction sector activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week through sector rotation and earnings revisions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with HOUST increases, or the signal's out-of-sample R-squared falls below 0.01 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95 percent of its 2017 baseline signals severe capacity contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in at least 60 percent of triggered instances, or the threshold effect loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward testing.
